title: Velvety Buttery Dates Pilaf
description: A creamy and flavorful date-night dish featuring dried red chillies for a spicy touch, ideal for Jain-friendly palates.

ingredients.name: 4-6 dried red chillies (adjust for spice preference)

instructions: Rinse the rice and soak it in water for at least an hour.

Drain the soaked rice and rinse again thoroughly. Add about 2 cups of water to the soaking water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to simmer until the water evaporates, leaving only the rice with a small amount of moisture.
Heat butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in dates, chopped red chillies, and coconut milk. Bring to a gentle simmer for about 5 minutes.
Add salt and lemon zest (if using) to taste, then add the cooked rice. Mix well until all rice is coated evenly with the mixture.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and let it cool slightly before serving.
